# EncryptSecret

> End-to-end encrypted secret sharing. Messages, passwords, and code snippets
> that self-destruct after reading. No account needed. No server ever sees plaintext.

## What is EncryptSecret?

EncryptSecret is a free, open privacy tool that lets anyone share sensitive
information securely over the internet without leaving a permanent trail.
It was built by Amos Isaiah Tizhe under the OneXportal brand.

All encryption happens in the user's browser using AES-256-GCM (Web Crypto API)
before any data is transmitted. The server stores only encrypted ciphertext.
Even the developer cannot read the content of any note.

## Tools

### EncryptSecret Notes
URL: https://encryptsecret.netlify.app/
Share private messages, sensitive text, or any secret that should vanish after
the recipient reads it. Burn-after-reading. Optional password. Expiry from 1 hour
to 7 days. AES-256-GCM encrypted in the browser.

### EncryptPass
URL: https://encryptsecret.netlify.app/encryptpass.html
Share passwords, API keys, PINs, and credentials securely via a one-time link.
Designed to replace sending passwords over WhatsApp, email, or SMS where they
persist in chat history forever. Self-destructs immediately after the recipient
opens the link.

### Private Pastebin
URL: https://encryptsecret.netlify.app/pastebin.html
Share encrypted code snippets, config files, error logs, and text up to 20,000
characters. Unlike public pastebins, content is encrypted before storage and
deleted permanently after reading. No account required.

### Help & Support
URL: https://encryptsecret.netlify.app/support.html
FAQ, step-by-step usage guide, and contact information.

## Key Features

- AES-256-GCM encryption performed entirely in the browser
- Zero knowledge — server never receives or stores plaintext
- Burn-after-reading — content deleted the moment it is opened
- Optional lock password for additional protection
- Passwordless notes use a random cryptographic key embedded in the URL hash
- URL hash key never transmitted to the server (browser security standard)
- Automatic expiry from 1 hour to 7 days
- Rolling rate limit — max 3 creations per 60 seconds per IP
- No user accounts, no sign-up, no email required
- No ads, no tracking, no analytics
- Content Security Policy headers enforced
- Row Level Security on the database
- Auto-deletion of all data after 8 days via scheduled database job
- Built on Supabase (PostgreSQL) and hosted on Netlify

## Use Cases

- Sharing a Wi-Fi password securely with a guest
- Sending database credentials to a colleague without exposing them in Slack
- Sharing API keys, tokens, or secrets during onboarding
- Sharing sensitive personal information (ID numbers, account details) that should vanish
- Developers sharing code snippets that contain sensitive configuration
- Sending a private message that leaves no trace on either device after reading
- Secure whistleblowing or sensitive disclosures

## Technical Details

- Frontend: HTML, CSS, Vanilla JavaScript (ES Modules)
- Encryption: Web Crypto API — AES-GCM 256-bit, PBKDF2 key derivation (310,000 iterations)
- Database: Supabase (PostgreSQL) with Row Level Security
- Hosting: Netlify with CSP security headers
- No backend server — static files only
- Rate limiting: client-side localStorage + server-side IP check via Netlify function

## Author

Name: Amos Isaiah Tizhe
Brand: OneXportal

Email: amosisaiahtizhe@gmail.com
Country: Nigeria

## Alternatives EncryptSecret improves upon

- privnote.com — no open source, closed ecosystem
- onetimesecret.com — server-side encryption only
- paste.ee — no encryption
- Regular Pastebin — completely public, no encryption
- Sharing passwords via WhatsApp/SMS/Email — persistent, unencrypted

## License

Open source. Built for public good. Free to use.
